assertions.title: Session persistence — list and load Pi sessions from ~/.pi/agent/sessions/

steps.observed: 2 tests passed; session entries correctly parsed from ls output including filename extraction and path handling; empty output yields empty list
3 tests passed; SessionInfo correctly constructed from PiSessionMetadata with first prompt as title; falls back to session ID when no prompt

steps.expected: 4 tests pass: parse_metadata_complete_session, parse_metadata_empty_session, parse_metadata_malformed_lines_skipped, parse_metadata_multiple_prompts
steps.observed: 4 tests passed; complete session parsed with session_id, first_prompt, message_count, line_count; empty session returns fallback ID; malformed lines skipped gracefully; multiple prompts correctly captures first prompt only
1 test passed; Pi provider transport correctly delegates list_sessions through the provider trait

assertions.title: Auto-detection — probe SSH host for pi binary and pi-acp availability

steps.expected: 4 tests pass: scenario_a_native_only, scenario_b_both, scenario_c_neither, partial_native_binary_no_version
steps.observed: 4 tests passed; Scenario A (native only) correctly detects PiNative only; Scenario B (both) detects both Native and ACP; Scenario C (neither) detects nothing with empty transports; partial detection (binary found but no version) correctly reports no transport

assertions.title: Pi provider selection and transport preference — per-server configuration

steps.expected: 14 tests pass covering all preference modes, fallback, forced modes, and display
steps.observed: 14 tests passed; PreferNative selects native when available, falls back to ACP; PreferAcp selects ACP when available, falls back to native; ForceNative errors when native unavailable; ForceAcp errors when ACP unavailable; default preference is PreferNative; display formatting correct for all variants; recomputation on availability change works correctly

steps.expected: 5 tests pass covering mock transport operations
steps.observed: 5 tests passed; mock channel supports queue/read, capture/write, reset, disconnect simulation, and pi-not-found scenario
0 tests matched this specific filter; all preference tests are covered under the broader 'preference' filter (14 tests above)
0 tests matched this specific filter; mock tests are covered under the 'mock_channel' filter (5 tests above)

summary: Tested 3 assertions: all 3 passed. VAL-PI-007 (10 tests across 4 runs): session persistence correctly parses ls output, metadata, and delegates through provider transport. VAL-PI-008 (4 tests): auto-detection correctly identifies all four probe scenarios (native-only, both, neither, partial). VAL-PI-015 (19 tests across 3 runs): transport preference selection covers all 4 preference modes with correct fallback behavior, forced mode errors, default preference, and mock channel infrastructure. Total: 33 tests, 0 failures.
